title: Rotting Oranges: Multi-Source BFS Infection

animation.explanation: Reveal the initial grid while preserving the distinction between empty, fresh, and rotten cells.
Mark each rotten orange as it enters the BFS queue, including every initial infection source.
Pulse the rotten orange currently removed from the queue and processed as an infection source.
Show the traced fresh cell becoming rotten at its recorded minute.
Reveal the traced minimum time, or show impossibility when fresh oranges remain.

intro.text: Fresh oranges are orange, rotten oranges are purple, and empty cells are dark.
All initially rotten oranges begin in the BFS queue at minute 0.

educational_notes: The queue starts with every initially rotten orange, so infection spreads from all sources simultaneously.
A dequeue pulse identifies the orange whose four neighboring cells are being examined.
The queue glow shows the BFS frontier; event order remains the exact FIFO order supplied by the trace.
Each cell_update is the factual moment a fresh orange becomes rotten, and its trace minute represents the elapsed infection time.
The final reveal uses the done event: it displays the elapsed minutes when no fresh oranges remain, -1 when infection is impossible, and 0 when no spreading is needed.
